standards.name: ML-KEM (Module-Lattice-Based Key-Encapsulation Mechanism)
standards.notes: Primary KEM standard. ML-KEM-768 recommended for most applications.

standards.name: ML-DSA (Module-Lattice-Based Digital Signature Algorithm)
standards.notes: Primary signature standard. ML-DSA-65 recommended for most applications.

standards.name: SLH-DSA (Stateless Hash-Based Digital Signature Algorithm)
standards.notes: Hash-based signatures. Conservative choice with well-understood security. Larger signatures than ML-DSA.

standards.name: FN-DSA (FFT over NTRU-Lattice-Based Digital Signature Algorithm)
standards.notes: Compact signatures. Based on FALCON. Not yet finalized.

transition_guidelines.name: Transitioning the Use of Cryptographic Algorithms and Key Lengths
key_requirements: 112-bit security minimum for all symmetric operations

transition_guidelines.name: Recommendation for Key Management
key_requirements: AES-128: acceptable (128-bit security, Grover reduces to 64-bit quantum)
AES-256: recommended (256-bit security, Grover reduces to 128-bit quantum)

transition_guidelines.name: NSA Commercial National Security Algorithm Suite 2.0

quantum_threat_timeline.harvest_now_decrypt_later: Active threat — adversaries collecting encrypted traffic today
